gaps.description: The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights per the Relationship to the Bill of Rights clause. The BoR is the source of enterprise scope (Article I), prohibited activities (Article II), core identity (Article III), and human authority reservation (Article IV). All six articles are marked '[To be defined by human principal].' Without a substantive BoR, Arbiter (#190) has nothing to enforce at the scope gate, and Nomos (#200) cannot adjudicate any Action Proposal against a meaningful perimeter. The entire governance stack — Arbiter scope gate, Nomos MSP+genome gate, Senate amendment authority, Cerberus audit — operates against a null constitution.

The MSP Rules section explicitly states: 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' While this is intentional at seed, the world state shows no substantive rules have been published since the April 10, 2026 bootstrap. The msp_version count is 1 (seed only). No genome documents exist ('none at seed'). Nomos (#200) has nothing to evaluate at the MSP+genome compliance gate, meaning all APs that clear the (null) Arbiter scope gate proceed unreviewed.

The Radiant stats show scheduled=disabled, last_dream=null, health=never_run, and a watchdog that has never checked in. The dream cycle is the mechanism by which Radiant consolidates context blocks into memory, detects patterns, and surfaces gaps. Without it, the system cannot learn from operational history, cannot detect recurring failure patterns (like the SafeVault backup failures or organ heartbeat instability), and cannot generate the insights that would trigger PEM creation or inform Senate of needed MSP amendments.

The Spine state transition log shows every organ oscillating between ALIVE and DEGRADED on approximately 30-second cycles (missed_pong → pong_recovered → missed_pong). This affects all 26+ organs including the governance-critical ones: Arbiter (#190), Nomos (#200), Senate (#220), and Cerberus. If governance organs are DEGRADED when an Action Proposal arrives or when Nomos needs to issue a PEM, adjudication fails or is bypassed. The MSP's two-gate architecture assumes organs are ALIVE and responsive.

The Recent Goals array is empty (`[]`). There is no evidence that any Action Proposal has been dispatched, evaluated by Arbiter, or adjudicated by Nomos since the MSP bootstrap on April 10, 2026. The entire governance pipeline — AP emission → Arbiter scope gate → Nomos MSP+genome gate → PEM generation → Senate-Legislator drafting → HOM notification → Senate-Rollout activation — has never been exercised end-to-end. The MSP describes a sophisticated amendment protocol that has never been triggered.

The BoR scaffold has awaited human authorship since the April 10 bootstrap (23 days). The MSP amendment protocol requires HOM notification to the 'human-principal' mailbox and a `POST /amend/review` with `decision=approve` for activation. Minder shows the human principal (Leon Cohen-Levy) was last seen on April 2, 2026 — before the MSP bootstrap. No human-authored BoR articles have been registered. The constitutional identity of the DIO remains undefined because the sole authorized author (human-principal-only per BoR authority field) has not written it.

The SafeVault backup system shows a pattern of failures spanning April 5 to May 3: script bugs (unbound variable), NAS mount failures (recurring across 4+ dates), zombie processes blocking SQLite backups (May 3 — two 25-hour zombie processes from previous day), and S3 partial completions. The MSP provides no operational rules because it is empty (GAP-002), but if it contained safety rules or invariants around data durability, these repeated backup failures would violate them. This represents an ungoverned operational risk to platform data integrity.

The MSP describes a full versioning protocol: hash-verified via Graph adapter, append-only msp_version concepts, status transitions (active → superseded). Graph shows exactly one msp_version concept (the seed). The Version History table lists only 1.0.0-seed with triggering PER '— (bootstrap).' Since the seed publication on April 10, no Policy Exception Message has triggered the amendment pipeline, no Senate-Legislator draft has entered review, and no new msp_version has been written. The versioning infrastructure exists but is idle.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-001",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "catastrophic",
      "description": "Scope of Enterprise is entirely undefined, leaving the organism with no boundary on permissible activities.",
      "rationale": "Without a defined scope, Nomos cannot reject Action Proposals that fall outside intended enterprise boundaries. The organism is effectively unconstrained in what it may attempt to do, violating the constitutional requirement of a bounded operational identity."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-002",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "catastrophic",
      "description": "Prohibited Activities are entirely undefined; there is no list of actions the organism must never perform.",
      "rationale": "Article II is the primary safety rail. Its absence means no action—no matter how harmful—can be constitutionally blocked. All other BoR articles depend on this negative definition of inviolable boundaries."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-003",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article III — Core Identity",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "critical",
      "description": "Core Identity is entirely undefined; the organism has no statement of what it fundamentally is.",
      "rationale": "Identity anchors the organism’s purpose, stable properties, and self-model. Without it, the organism cannot self-regulate against drift, cannot explain its own nature, and cannot resist manipulation or mission creep."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-004",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "critical",
      "description": "Human Authority Reservation is entirely undefined; the limits of agentic autonomy vs. human control are not spelled out.",
      "rationale": "The DIO must be subordinate to human principals, but without a clear reservation, there is no constitutional backstop against fully autonomous decisions that should require human approval. This risks both safety and trust."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-005",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "high",
      "description": "Amendment Protocol is entirely undefined; there is no secure process to evolve the Bill of Rights.",
      "rationale": "The BoR must remain adaptable under strict human control, but without a defined protocol, any future attempt to amend it is undefined and could be contested or executed incorrectly, freezing the constitution or introducing instability."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "BOR-006",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "description": "Interpretation Rules are entirely undefined; Arbiter (#190) has no guidance for resolving ambiguities.",
      "rationale": "Even a well-written BoR will contain edge cases. Without interpretation rules, Arbiter must rely on ad-hoc reasoning, potentially inconsistent enforcement of constitutional boundaries, and increased risk of undetected scope violations."
    }
  ]
}
